Divine Intervention  

Description

1: Death Save - Restore Full Health
3: Lycanthropy

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 500 Skill: Abjuration
Casting Time: 6 Recast Time: 90
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Range: 100
Location: Any Time of Day: Any
Deletable: No Blockable: Yes
Focusable: Yes Dispellable: Yes
Interruptable: Yes Short Buff Box: No
Timer: 7 Target Type: Single
Spell Type: Beneficial Source: Live 12/03

Messages

You cast: You invoke the presence of the gods.
Other cast: Soandso calls upon the gods.
Cast on you: You feel the watchful eyes of the gods upon you.
Cast on other: Soandso feels the watchful eyes of the gods upon them.
Effect Fades: You are no longer watched.

Game Description

Bestows the favor of the gods upon your target, granting them a second chance at life should they fall in battle. Consumes a pair of emeralds when cast.

Reagents

  • Emerald x 2

Items with this effect

  • Spell: Divine Intervention

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Divine Intervention
ClassLevel
CLR60
Expansion:

Duration:
10.0 mins
  • Comments (34)
  • Sold By (1)
  • Dropped By (18)
  • Crafted (1)

The Plane of Knowledge
  • Vicar Delin - 117pp 6gp 6sp 5cp

▲ Page top
DI is the bomb
QuoteReply
# Apr 16 2006 at 3:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591877787914
12 posts
Since my main is cleric 70 and my husband is a 70 warrior we've had alot of chances to test this spell.

Do spend your AA's in Unfailing Divinity, I have noticed 10fold that my DI goes off nearly 100% of the time when put on another player no matter what their CHA is at.
▲ Page top
spell
QuoteReply
# Apr 15 2005 at 8:42 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591802408254
View User Forum
6 posts
is this spell able to be researched ? i am told it can be researched yet its not listed as so.Any ifno please.
▲ Page top
RE: spell
QuoteReply
# May 05 2005 at 11:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591801435431
Scholar
105 posts
Sure can be, got a nice necro to do mine when I needed it about a year ago... components needed are:

a piece of parchment and a quill (same for all spells)

part of toharon's memoir pg. 21 (left)
toharon's memoir pg. 22
words of recourse
▲ Page top
question
QuoteReply
# Nov 03 2004 at 4:38 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592763541623
7 posts
Since the OOW expansion does this still cost 2 emeralds? or did they do away with the jewels like they did for all the other buffs.
▲ Page top
RE: question
QuoteReply
# Dec 22 2004 at 5:30 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593363223165
25 posts
Yup. My fiance's cleric goes through emeralds by the bag full. This is why clerics are loot whores - they sell the stuff and buy emeralds! =D
▲ Page top
DI and UD
QuoteReply
# Sep 06 2004 at 6:34 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1644193099274
View User Forum
31 posts
Let's not get Divine Intervention (spell) and Unfailing Divinity (AA) confused here. DI cast on your target's chances of going off factors in the buffed person's charisma, not the cleric. There are many places to find trials of DI success/failure based on charisma bearing this out. From my own personal experience once the tank has 150+ charisma it should go off reliably enough not to second guess the cost of the emeralds. With AA's and extended duration focus this buff lasts for 16 minutes and heals a max of 8k hp. Let's not forget that it came from the Kunark era and our tanks didn't have the hps they do now.
As far as UD is concerned, this AA has saved my life more times than I can count. Before putting in the AA's to get this DI would kick in a good portion of the time. Once I'd gotten all the other AA's worth anything I went back and got UD. Granted the tank I use it on has 160 charisma, but DI kicks in atleast 90% of the time when I use it on him. That's just my 2 copper...take it for what it's worth ;)

Edited, Sat Oct 2 21:59:57 2004
▲ Page top
RE: DI and UD
QuoteReply
# Sep 06 2004 at 6:59 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255473800
Scholar
619 posts
err, think you may be confusing Unfailing Divinity and Touch of the Divine
▲ Page top
page 22
QuoteReply
# Aug 29 2004 at 10:31 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591780763843
39 posts
22 dropped of mosquito in ct today ( boots of flowing slime camp )
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 30 2004 at 7:41 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This spell was named after the Slayer CD "Divine Intervention"
DI
QuoteReply
# Apr 16 2004 at 3:48 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361488497
View User Forum
3 posts
Divine Intervention doesn't work well unless the caster (cleric) has the AA Unfailing Divinity 3. With that AA maxed, the chance that DI works to save the person you've buffed with it goes up. It still won't always work, but usually works.
▲ Page top
RE: DI
QuoteReply
# May 01 2004 at 10:01 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591795362173
293 posts
I would like to point out your statement DOESN'T WORK WELL...

I have to agree. I would suggest if you are a younger cleric without the AA's to blow on Unfailing Divinity, DON'T DO IT ! Your AA's are better spent elsewhere. I being one of those clerics wish I could get back what I have spent.

Stick with Death Pact on the Enchanter. Cheaper,Longer duration and heals just enough for me to save the chanter from going down.

Just my opinion, I could be wrong.
▲ Page top
RE: DI
QuoteReply
# May 07 2004 at 10:59 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591806316023
Scholar
50 posts
If you're planning on fighting a big mob, and you're the only healer, and there's only one tanking class. Divine Intervention is incredibly useful, and as this is your average group layout - Do spend your AA's in Unfailing Divinity.

A touch of the Divine;

Edited, Fri May 7 12:00:24 2004
▲ Page top
still no dice after 20
QuoteReply
# Apr 06 2004 at 3:35 PM Rating: Decent
Lariatha
93 posts
20 casts, none saved yet, charisma on me, 186 (with buffage before cast). MT/RT/SA only get this buff before raids, and it has a terrible recast time. Always cast charisma buffs on MT/RT. I've only seen death pact work tbh, and it's worked everytime.. just not really an efficient heal in raid encounters. nor is the buffer it provides when mobs are hitting for a min of 1054 :) Someone please tell me if they get this to work!
____________________________
cleric, maelin <retired 05>

DI works with CHR
QuoteReply
# Feb 12 2004 at 2:45 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592756983001
5 posts
Actually i Beleive the Chances go up with the Chraisma of the caster not the person that the spell is cast upon please post if you are for sure wether i am right or wrong. And a Quick Question how did a Paladin have a CHR of (15)

Edited, Thu Feb 12 14:47:03 2004
▲ Page top
RE: DI works with CHR
QuoteReply
# Feb 22 2004 at 1:43 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591779076290
11 posts
Charisma check goes for the target not the caster, so eventhough I'm a dorf with 48 CHA, it doesn't matter. As long as the target has decent Charisma it works.
Thor
▲ Page top
Cha
QuoteReply
# Jan 08 2004 at 10:30 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591796354124
36 posts
The higher cha you have the better chance of DI activating on you. Before i quit we would always cast DI on the MT so if there was a slip in the CH rotation it would save him/her. It would fail for the MA probally once out of every 15 chances with around 200+ cha. For me, I had very low cha (15) it almost never worked for me. Probally once out of every 10 with 15cha.

Retired Paladin of Prexus
"Every Day is a Good Day"

Death Touch
QuoteReply
# Dec 11 2003 at 2:51 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592255384524
103 posts
Death Touch does 21k damage instantly. So DI won't fire because there is too much overdamage.
▲ Page top
Wrong
QuoteReply
# Nov 17 2003 at 10:26 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592257493135
321 posts
wrong, DI hits when u after the first attack that sends u below 15% hps. Heals for 8k
▲ Page top
Buffs?
QuoteReply
# Oct 03 2003 at 11:43 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592760143674
77 posts
Does this spell also retain all buffs that were prevviously on you before your Divine Intervention saved yer *****
▲ Page top
RE: Buffs?
QuoteReply
# Oct 03 2003 at 1:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592760607996
Scholar
28 posts
Yes. When you reach 0 Hp and Divine intervention is activated it act like a complete heal. You keep all buffs just like if you never died.

If i remember well, when spell effect is activated, its says TARGET been revived by Divine Intervention. This spell is very helpfull when you try hight level mobs. If one of your healers go link dead or lag slow the heal proces, this spell your second chance.

Worth the 2 emeralds cost to save a raid.
▲ Page top
DT?
QuoteReply
# Oct 02 2003 at 7:59 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592758693828
39 posts
Does Divine Intervention work on Death Touch? =)
▲ Page top
RE: DT?
QuoteReply
# Oct 03 2003 at 8:22 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592760607996
Scholar
28 posts
DI works when you reach 0 HP. It will work on harm touch and will even on a 20k hit point damages from falling.

DI works most of the time. There is still a chance that it will not work to.
▲ Page top
death pack
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2003 at 11:11 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591775671918
1 post
I still perfer Death Pack, chances are the same for an out of body experience, and I might get a heal off before the mob kills them again. All in all, death pack has worked fairly well, and last alot longer, uses only a peal, and cost much less in mana
▲ Page top
OK this is what I know
QuoteReply
# Jul 12 2003 at 7:02 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251709006
145 posts
When you cast this buff on someone. It requires two emerald components. Duration of buff is 10min. NOW when that someone who has this buff will get a % chance of Divine Intervention if they are killed ( Its like you blessed the bastage before the battle and asked your god to watch over them )
Anyway lets suppose your friend dies and the gods took pity on his soul ( I.E. he made his % save vs death ) Your friend would get a Complete heal and <poof> be alive once again only to die another horrible death because your oom from casting this spell on everyone and they didnt wait for you to get to full before the pull and that damn tank went afk again to get a soda then started watching MTV 'punked' before he remembered that he was playing EQ.......... Hu oh sorry bout that. Anyway that is what I know. Hope it helped.

Edited, Sat Jul 12 19:55:57 2003
▲ Page top
How does this work?
QuoteReply
# Jun 29 2003 at 2:53 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591771658957
22 posts
Can someone explain exactly how this spell works, does it mean you cant die for 10min? Does it just keep healing the target for 10min or what?
▲ Page top
RE: How does this work?
QuoteReply
# Jan 08 2004 at 12:41 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591789799503
156 posts
Means that if you are reduced to 0 or less hitpoints there is a chance that you will receive an 8k heal. From my experiences it is like the player never died, they don't fall down, they retain agro and buffs. This spell requires two emeralds to cast.
▲ Page top
Percent chance?
QuoteReply
# Mar 01 2003 at 2:20 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592254254545
View User Forum
56 posts
What's the percentage of a heal upon death with this buff on?
▲ Page top
RE: Percent chance?
QuoteReply
# Dec 30 2003 at 11:37 PM Rating: Decent
CentaurCav
64 posts
Contained in my log i have casted DI 108 times and it has saved my MT 5 times to (I would say) 30 MT deaths
I have to think this spell is well below 25% chance. I tend to DI MT / RT and I know I have lost MT at least 20 times w/ a total of 5 saves.
I have never tested it more in depth than that =(
▲ Page top
RE: Percent chance?
QuoteReply
# Mar 13 2003 at 9:58 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592759896364
4 posts
Full healt, its like you cast a lay hand on target, you loose the buff and gain all your HP.
Use 2 emerald, and only 10min, but is great to pull hard mobs.
